description: Per-file comment/doc counts for LLM batch targeting. Rust: lines_triple_slash=///, lines_inner_doc=//!, lines_plain_comment=// excluding doc. Markdown: lines_total=all lines; lines_other_doc_signal=# heading count. hotspot_tier: 1=plan-listed path, 2=high-density heuristic, 0=other. symbol_hints (schema v3): plan-hotspot Rust only; /// or //! linked to next item; containing_symbol, doc_preview, comment_type, quality_tag (mechanical|operational|section_divider|user_help|narrative|ssot_sensitive).

MCP tools: episodic memory, knowledge graph queries, and session-scoped recall.
**Agents calling these tools:** Use stable, namespaced `key` strings for writes; pair
`MemoryStoreParams::agent_id` with the orchestrator’s agent id when available. Cap graph
fan-out with [`KnowledgeQueryParams::limit`](crate::memory::KnowledgeQueryParams::limit) so tool responses stay bounded. For authoritative
Codex semantics, see repo-root `AGENTS.md` §2.2.1 and ADR 004—not every tool doc repeats the
data-plane glossary.

Real-time event bus for agent activity broadcasting.
Publishes structured `AgentEvent`s over a tokio broadcast channel.
Consumers (dashboard SSE, monitors, gamify hooks) subscribe and receive
events as they happen — no polling, no JSONL heuristics.
Monotonically increasing event ID.
What an agent is currently doing.
Writing code / editing files.
Reading / searching files.
Running a command or tool.
Thinking / planning (waiting for LLM response).
Waiting for user input or permission.
Idle — no active task.
A structured event emitted by the orchestrator.
Each event carries a unique ID, timestamp, and typed payload.
This replaces Pixel Agents' heuristic-based JSONL parsing with
deterministic, structured events.
Unique event identifier.
Unix timestamp in milliseconds.
Event payload.
The different kinds of events the orchestrator can emit.
A new agent was spawned.
An agent was retired/removed.
An agent's activity changed.
A task was submitted to the queue.
Optional session link (for chat/workflow grouping in Mens).
A task started executing.
Optional session link (for chat/workflow grouping in Mens).
A task completed successfully.
Optional session link (for chat/workflow grouping in Mens).
A task failed.
Optional session link (for chat/workflow grouping in Mens).
A file lock was acquired.
A file lock was released.
An agent went idle (no pending tasks).
An agent started working again.
An inter-agent message was sent.
A cost was incurred (LLM API call).
**MCP:** when Codex is attached, persisted usage is SSOT in `provider_usage`; bus emission is
gated by **`VOX_MCP_LLM_COST_EVENTS`** (default off with DB) to avoid dashboards double-counting.
Structured temporal context (date, server_idle_secs)
Auto-continuation was triggered for an idle agent.
A plan handoff between agents.
A scope violation was detected.
Context window compaction was triggered.
Pre-compaction memory flush completed.
A new session was created.
A session was reset.
Workspace / DB snapshot captured for undo or conflict tracking.
Optional session link (for chat/workflow grouping in Mens).
Overlapping edits detected between agents.
Undo stack applied.
Redo stack applied.
Handoff could not be completed (e.g. spawn failure).
Handoff completed; target agent resumed work.
Load balancer moved tasks due to urgent queue depth.
Streaming LLM token chunk (debug / UI).
Prompt injection / safety gate rejected input (MCP).
Canonicalized prompt produced warnings (MCP task submit).
Planning router chose a strategy for a submitted goal.
A new plan session was created.
A branch/replan version was created.
Failure triggered a replan branch.
Planner requested workflow runtime handoff.
Workflow handoff finished and yielded a task id.
Durable workflow lifecycle (MCP / dashboard).
Endpoint reliability observation from LLM call (feeds EWMA in Codex).
Provider endpoint URL.
Model identifier.
Proxy signal for hallucination risk (0.0–1.0).
Ratio of contradictory claims detected (0.0–1.0).
1.0 for infra failures (rate-limit/timeout), 0.0 otherwise.
True when the failure was a rate-limit response.
True when the call timed out.
The entire orchestrator (all agents) has been idle.
Milliseconds of absolute silence across all agents.
A task was timed out and removed from the queue.
Expired task ID.
Agent ID that was holding it.
Age in milliseconds.

Central coordinator for queues, affinity, locks, scope, and JJ-style undo metadata.
[`Orchestrator`] is the integration point for routing services, Codex-backed stores,
and runtime agent processes when the `runtime` feature is enabled.
## Sub-module structure (decomposed from the original god-object)
| Sub-module | Responsibility |
| [`core`] | `new`, `with_groups`, `init_db`, `record_ai_usage` |
| [`agent_lifecycle`] | `spawn_agent`, `retire_agent`, `pause/resume`, `heartbeat`, `handoff` |
| [`scaling`] | `rebalance`, `tick` |
| [`vcs_ops`] | `capture_snapshot`, `take_db_snapshot`, `undo/redo_operation` |

Session lifecycle management for Vox agents.
Inspired by OpenClaw's session model:
- When a DB is attached, **`agent_sessions` + `agent_session_events` in Codex** are the durable SSOT.
- JSONL under [`SessionConfig::sessions_dir`] is an **optional, non-authoritative** export when `persist` is enabled (telemetry / human-readable traces — not replay truth).
- Each session has its own context, permissions, and state.
- Supports reset, cleanup, idle timeout, and daily reset policies.
- Restarts should reload from Codex via [`SessionManager::load`]; JSONL replay is legacy fallback only ([`SessionManager::load_from_jsonl`]).
Lifecycle state of a session.
Actively being used.
Idle — no recent activity.
Has been compacted (history summarized).
Archived — can be cleaned up.
A JSONL event appended to the session file.
A single conversation turn stored in session history.

Conversation history (cleared on reset, pruned on compaction).
Arbitrary per-session key-value metadata.
Per-plugin persistent state.
Total turns ever added (monotonic, not reset on compaction).
Total tokens ever used (monotonic).
Create a new session for the given agent.

Milliseconds since this message was created.
HF file checks before native Candle QLoRA (`vox schola train --backend qlora`).
**Tokenizer contract:** `--backend qlora` requires [`MensTokenizerMode::Hf`] and a real
Hugging Face `tokenizer.json` (download via `--model <repo>` or explicit path). The Burn LoRA
path (`--backend lora`) uses the Vox tokenizer stack — do not mix modes; CLI enforces this before
training dispatches.
**Weights:** requires HF `config.json` plus safetensors shards listing a supported **embedding**
matrix (`wte.weight` or `model.embed_tokens.weight`) for vocab / `d_model` discovery passed into
[`super::candle_qlora_train`]. Full training graph details: see module docs on
`candle_qlora_train.rs` and mdBook [`mens-training-ssot.md`](../../../../docs/src/architecture/mens-training-ssot.md).
Prefer `wte` (GPT-2) before `embed_tokens` (Llama/Qwen) when both exist anywhere in the shard list.
Scan all shards: first **valid** rank-2 table in key order (`wte` then `embed_tokens`).
If a preferred key exists but is not rank-2, fail (do not fall back silently).
Resolved embedding table metadata for the Candle trainer.
HF `config.json` path (used for layout parse and tracing; keep for operator debugging).
HF `config.json` layout (layers, heads, model family).
Fail fast unless HF tokenizer + safetensors shards contain a supported embedding matrix.
